assertion_results.text: Output must score at least three areas at 4/5 or higher
assertion_results.evidence: CI/CD 5/5, Testing 4/5, Code review 4/5, Monitoring 5/5, Developer Experience 5/5, Ownership & culture 4/5 — six of six areas score 4/5 or higher.

assertion_results.text: Output must not recommend tooling the team already has (e.g. Datadog, feature flags, SLOs)
assertion_results.evidence: No recommendation to adopt Datadog, SLOs, or feature flags. The medium-term item 'Introduce a feature flag lifecycle review: monthly async scan of active flags' is a process recommendation for managing existing flags, not a suggestion to adopt the tooling. No new monitoring or observability tooling from the team's existing stack is proposed.

assertion_results.text: Output must give at least one non-trivial medium- or long-term recommendation
assertion_results.evidence: Medium term: 'Evaluate contract testing (e.g., Pact) for B2B SaaS integrations. At Series B with external customers, API contracts are a real integration risk worth guarding against.' Long term: 'Consider mutation testing (e.g., Stryker for TypeScript) on critical modules to validate that the 2,100 unit tests are actually catching real bugs — not just executing code paths.'

assertion_results.text: Output must acknowledge the low incident rate of 0.1 P1/month
assertion_results.evidence: The specific figure '0.1 P1/month' does not appear anywhere in the report. The only incident-related mention is: 'MTTR of 12 minutes is excellent; capturing the "why" on even low-frequency incidents compounds learning at the team level.' — this references low frequency vaguely but never cites the 0.1 P1/month figure.

assertion_results.text: Output must include at least one improvement item
assertion_results.evidence: The report contains an 'Improvement plan' section with 11 action items (A1–A11) across short, medium, and long-term horizons, e.g. 'A1 | Audit and triage all skipped/disabled tests', 'A8 | Evaluate contract testing (Pact) for B2B API boundaries', 'A10 | Pilot mutation testing (Stryker) on highest-risk modules'.
